sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.content: In 1993 Dan Pearman went to Ecuador
options: A as a tourist guide
B as part of his studies
C as a voluntary worker

questions.content: Dan's neighbour was successful in business because he
options: A employed carpenters from the area
B was the most skilled craftsman in the town
C found it easy to reach customers

questions.content: Dan says the charity relies on
options: A getting enough bicycles to send regularly
B finding new areas which need the bicycles
C charging for the bicycles it sends abroad

questions.content: What does Dan say about the town of Rivas?
options: A It has received the greatest number of bikes
B It has almost as many bikes as Amsterdam
C Its economy has been totally transformed

questions.content: What problem did the charity face in August 2000?
options: A It couldn't meet its overheads
B It had to delay sending the bikes
C It was criticized in the British media

questions.content:
How much money did the charity receive when it won an award? {{BLANK}}
What is the charity currently hoping to buy? {{BLANK}}
Which THREE things can the general public do to help the charity Pedal Power?
options: A organise a bicycle collection
B repair the donated bikes
C donate their unwanted tools
D do voluntary work in its office
E hold an event to raise money
F identify areas that need bikes
G write to the government

sections.instruction: Choose the table below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

questions.content:
GEOGRAPHY
• more than (31) {{BLANK}}  times as big as the UK
• most of the area is classified as (32) {{BLANK}}
RESEARCH STATIONS
• (33) {{BLANK}}  is integrated with technical support
• stations contain accommodation, work areas, a kitchen, a (34) {{BLANK}}  and a gym
• supplies were brought to Zero One station by sledge from a (35) {{BLANK}}  at the edge of the ice 15 km away
• problem of snow build-ups solved by building stations on (36) {{BLANK}}  with adjustable legs
FOOD AND DIET
• average daily requirement for an adult in Antarctica is approximately (37) {{BLANK}}  kilocalories
• rations for field work prepared by process of freeze-drying
RESEARCH
The most important research focuses on climate change, including
– measuring changes in the ice-cap (because of effects on sea levels and (38) {{BLANK}}  )
– analysing air from bubbles in ice to measure (39) {{BLANK}}  caused by human activity
WORK OPPORTUNITIES
Many openings for (40) {{BLANK}}  people including
– research assistants, administrative and technical positions
